summary: api-rs-node ships a system-info exfiltration payload in clob.js
details: The package contains clob.js which combines child_process, fs, os, http(s) and a set of hardcoded IPFS gateway endpoints (violet-tricky-quelea-562.mypinata.cloud, gateway.pinata.cloud, cloudflare-ipfs.com) used to fetch follow-on content and to POST data outward. The file reads host identity via os.userInfo(), spawns child processes, performs filesystem reads via fs.existsSync, and issues outbound HTTPS GET / HTTP POST requests against the embedded gateway hostnames. This is the standard shape of a credential/host-info exfiltration loader: collect installer-side identity and filesystem state, fetch second-stage content from an IPFS-fronted endpoint, and POST results back. IPFS gateways are a common attacker pattern because the published content is mutable from the publisher side while looking like a CDN to scanners. The package's stated identity (a generic 'api' Node binding) does not justify any of these primitives.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
